description: M0 of the B-state roadmap: stand up the roadmap surface (docs/roadmap.md + a pinned roadmap epic + M0-M6 milestone issues) with M1 promoted to autopilot. No migration executed in this unit.

userStories.title: Create roadmap label and pinned epic issue
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I want a `roadmap` GitHub label and a pinned epic issue so the B-state vision has a canonical, discoverable tracking surface.
acceptanceCriteria: An epic issue carrying the `roadmap` label is open and pinned.
The epic body holds the north-star paragraph, the export-ness principle, and a checklist of M0-M6 linking each milestone issue.
No code, file, or migration artifact is changed by this story (GitHub-state only).
userStories.notes: Done in M0 setup: roadmap label created; epic #301 pinned with the M0-M6 checklist and export-ness principle.

userStories.title: File M0-M6 milestone issues with correct labels and dependency lines
userStories.description: As autopilot, I want M0-M6 issues filed with `roadmap` labels (and `autopilot` on M1) and explicit Blocked-by lines so issue selection respects dependency order.
acceptanceCriteria: Seven milestone issues exist (M0-M6), each `roadmap`-labeled, each referencing the epic.
The M1 issue additionally carries the `autopilot` label; M2-M6 do not.
Each milestone body states its Blocked-by relationship (M1,M2<-M0; M3<-M1; M4<-M3; M5,M6<-M1).
`gh issue list --label roadmap` returns the epic plus the seven milestones.
userStories.notes: Done in M0 setup: milestone issues #302-#308 filed, all roadmap-labeled; #303 (M1) also autopilot; each body carries its Blocked-by line.

userStories.title: Author docs/roadmap.md as a Docusaurus north-star page
userStories.description: As a contributor or autopilot session, I want docs/roadmap.md to exist as a well-formed Docusaurus page so the B-state north-star is surfaced in the docs site and linkable from the epic.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/roadmap.md` exists with YAML frontmatter (title, sidebar_position) matching the docs/intro.md style.

It contains the Vision, the export-ness axis, the three-namespace table (.mifune/ | .oh/ | root), the A-state->B-state collapse, the milestone table (M0-M6 with deps + status + issue links), and the maintenance pattern.
It links the epic issue and each milestone issue by number.
Markdown renders without unclosed code fences or broken tables (docs build does not error on the page).
userStories.notes: docs/roadmap.md authored (sidebar_position 8); 8 ordered sections, namespace table, and the M0-M6 milestone table with issue links; MDX-safe.

userStories.title: Wire docs/roadmap.md into the repo (CHANGELOG + epic backlink)
userStories.description: As a future agent reading session context, I want the roadmap page discoverable via CHANGELOG so the new surface is not silently orphaned.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG.md `## [Unreleased]` has an `### Added` entry for the roadmap page + milestone issues, referencing #302.
The docs sidebar resolves the new page (frontmatter sidebar_position is sufficient for the auto-generated sidebar; no manual registration needed).
The epic issue body links to docs/roadmap.md.
`bash evals/probes/wiki-readme-index.sh` still passes (no wiki drift introduced).
userStories.notes: CHANGELOG [Unreleased] Added entry references #302 (and #301, #302-#308); epic #301 body already backlinks docs/roadmap.md.
